  "weights": {

    "RX.2": 1.0,

    "RX.4": 0.3,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which focus should I prioritize for this section: (A) sentence-level polish and concision, (B) conceptual/logical tightening of the argument, (C) evidence–claim alignment and citation accuracy, or (D) section-level reorganization? You may choose multiple; if unspecified, I will default to A+B.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "Tightening the premise-to-conclusion chain around the scIB grounding and Harmony default ensures the argument is coherent and non-circular.",
    "R1.3": "Verifying that claims (e.g., benchmark-based justification and 18/18 pipeline execution) are supported by what is reported in this section aligns assertions with stated evidence.",
    "R1.6": "Sentence- and paragraph-level edits can reduce repetition, clarify phrasing, and improve readability without altering higher-level structure.",
    "RX.1": "Flagging where qualifiers are needed (e.g., around 'gold-standard' or default behaviors) helps communicate residual uncertainty.",
    "RX.2": "Careful phrasing will prevent overstating benchmark authority or generality of results beyond what the text supports.",
    "RX.3": "Work will be kept within the scope of assisting this section and the specified R1 dimensions only.",
    "RX.4": "Avoiding language that could encourage unsupported tool recommendations mitigates potential downstream misinterpretation.",
    "RX.5": "Any suggestions will be anchored to specific sentences and the cited source to maintain traceability."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which focus should I prioritize for this section: (A) sentence-level polish and concision, (B) conceptual/logical tightening of the argument, (C) evidence–claim alignment and citation accuracy, or (D) section-level reorganization? You may choose multiple; if unspecified, I will default to A+B.

rationale.R1.2: Tightening the premise-to-conclusion chain around the scIB grounding and Harmony default ensures the argument is coherent and non-circular.
rationale.R1.3: Verifying that claims (e.g., benchmark-based justification and 18/18 pipeline execution) are supported by what is reported in this section aligns assertions with stated evidence.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence- and paragraph-level edits can reduce repetition, clarify phrasing, and improve readability without altering higher-level structure.
rationale.RX.1: Flagging where qualifiers are needed (e.g., around 'gold-standard' or default behaviors) helps communicate residual uncertainty.
rationale.RX.2: Careful phrasing will prevent overstating benchmark authority or generality of results beyond what the text supports.
rationale.RX.3: Work will be kept within the scope of assisting this section and the specified R1 dimensions only.
rationale.RX.4: Avoiding language that could encourage unsupported tool recommendations mitigates potential downstream misinterpretation.
rationale.RX.5: Any suggestions will be anchored to specific sentences and the cited source to maintain traceability.
